fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Sidewalk Network
fr.considerations: Multiple Access Points; Fenced Sections; Dense Vegetation
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility of designated access points and ensuring they remain unobstructed by any entrance barriers could improve emergency responder access and movement. Review the fenced sections to ensure they do not impede a quick response, and consider installing gates that allow for easier navigation. Regular maintenance of the dense vegetation around the perimeter of the property may enhance visibility and prevent obstructions to pathways and important access routes. Clear signage could also facilitate responder navigation and staging, particularly if multiple access points are present. Maintaining clear and direct vehicle parking areas connecting to principal building entrances may assist with responder operations and streamline emergency vehicle staging. Having a well-marked and maintained sidewalk network could further support efficient responder movement throughout the property.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure. Managing the scattered shrubs and reducing their concentration, especially those adjacent to the structure, may help to decrease fuel continuity and reduce potential fire exposure to the building. The existing open terrain offers an opportunity to preserve and expand fuel breaks, which can act as buffers against fire spread. Maintaining clear zones within the vegetated perimeter will further support this effort, contributing to improved long-term wildfire resilience. Implementing regular vegetation management practices, such as pruning and clearing dead plant material, can enhance property preparedness and contribute to supporting firefighter access if needed.
